project.name: media-nest
project.description: MediaNest is an Android-first, offline-centric media library application designed for personal media consumption and organization. It allows you to download YouTube videos and audio locally, organize them into folders, maintain playback history, and optionally sync your metadata across devices. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

tour.title: Project Overview & Structure
tour.description: Welcome to MediaNest, an Android-first personal media consumption and organization library. The application allows users to catalog online links, stream media, download files for offline playback, extract audio tracks, and synchronize metadata across multiple devices. Start by reading the README to understand the core features, architectural layout, and setup instructions.

tour.title: Application Entry & Dependency Injection
tour.description: Understand the entry point of the Android application. MediaNestApp initializes the Hilt dependency injection container, which is used throughout the client code. MainActivity serves as the single-activity entry point, checking permissions and launching the root Compose navigation UI.
tour.languageLesson: In Android applications, Hilt simplifies dependency injection by generating Dagger components automatically. `@HiltAndroidApp` triggers code generation that creates a base class for your application containing a dependency container.

tour.title: UI Scaffolding & Navigation Graph
tour.description: Examine how the UI is laid out and navigated. MainScreen sets up the primary Jetpack Compose scaffolding, exposing a persistent bottom navigation bar and a floating draggable MiniPlayer. AppNavigation maps routes to Compose screens, keeping UI state isolated and clean.
tour.languageLesson: Jetpack Compose Navigation uses a NavHost controller. By defining routes as strings (or typed objects in newer Compose), you can dynamically swap screens and pass arguments while retaining navigation history.

tour.title: Local Database Scaffolding
tour.description: Investigate how MediaNest persists cataloged media offline. AppDatabase represents the local SQLite database managed by Room, defining entities and exposing Data Access Objects (DAOs). VideoEntity represents a saved media item, while VideoDao provides methods to query, favorite, search, or delete records.
tour.languageLesson: Room uses Kotlin Symbol Processing (KSP) to compile annotations like `@Database`, `@Entity`, and `@Dao` into standard SQLite CRUD operations. Returning a Flow from a DAO query instructs Room to automatically emit new updates when the underlying database table changes.

tour.title: Online Media Extraction
tour.description: Discover how the app parses streaming URLs. YouTubeExtractor wraps NewPipeExtractor to scrap video streams, playlists, and channel metadata without official API keys. DownloaderProvider configures connection properties and HTTP headers to prevent request throttling by content providers.
tour.languageLesson: Kotlin coroutines let you run long-running network operations, such as web scraping, on a background dispatcher (`Dispatchers.IO`) without blocking the UI main thread.

tour.title: Background Downloading Service
tour.description: Learn about the download pipeline that enables offline playback. DownloadService is an Android Foreground Service that coordinates multiple active media download threads. DownloadRepository bridges download status updates from the service to the local Room database to reflect real-time progress.
tour.languageLesson: Android Foreground Services require a persistent notification to inform the user that background work is active, preventing the operating system from aggressively terminating the process under resource constraints.

tour.title: FFmpeg Audio Extraction
tour.description: Explore post-processing operations on downloaded video streams. AudioExtractor attempts native demuxing of media files to extracting M4A audio. If that fails, it falls back to executing FFmpeg commands asynchronously to transcode the video stream into high-quality MP3 files.
tour.languageLesson: Running external command-line binaries like FFmpeg in Android requires executing sub-processes via standard Java Runtime APIs or wrapper libraries, handling standard output/error streams carefully to avoid thread deadlock.

tour.title: ExoPlayer Media Playback Core
tour.description: Study the media playback implementation. PlaybackService manages the MediaSession lifecycle, coordinating ExoPlayer streaming for HLS content and playing offline local media files. PlayerScreen binds the PlayerView to Compose, while PlayerViewModel coordinates active playback speed, position tracking, and history updates.
tour.languageLesson: ExoPlayer integrates with Android's MediaSession framework to coordinate playback controls, audio focus changes, lock screen media controls, and notification tray triggers automatically.

tour.title: FastAPI Sync Server API
tour.description: Switch context to the remote sync server. Written in Python using FastAPI, the sync-server lets multiple client devices backup and coordinate their library changes. The application entry point is main.py, database.py manages SQLite connections, and sync.py hosts the push/pull endpoints for change tracking.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI uses modern Python type hints and Pydantic validation to automatically parse JSON requests, generate OpenAPI documentation, and run routes asynchronously using uvicorn.

tour.title: Incremental Database Synchronization Client
tour.description: Learn how the Android client pushes data updates to the FastAPI server. SyncManager monitors local database changes, packages them into batch payloads, and handles server conflict resolution. SyncWorker runs periodically in the background using Android WorkManager to auto-sync modifications.
tour.languageLesson: WorkManager's CoroutineWorker lets you schedule asynchronous background tasks that execute even if the application process is terminated, supporting constraints like network connectivity and battery charging states.

tour.title: Sync Server Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: Understand how the sync-server is containerized for easy cloud deployment. Dockerfile builds a lightweight Alpine Python container using uv as a fast package installer. docker-compose.yml defines the FastAPI container, environment settings, port mappings, and persistent SQLite database volumes.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds allow you to isolate development dependencies and compilation utilities in early build stages, producing minimal production container images containing only runtime code.
